name: Resident Care & ACQSC Documentation (AU)
description: Reference policy aligned with the Strengthened Aged Care Quality Standards (2026) and the SIRS framework. Documentation here is examined directly by ACQSC assessors.

data.text: Resident care & documentation — Strengthened Standards aligned
Each resident has an individualised care plan reviewed at admission, on change of condition, and at least every 6 months — with the resident's voice documented.
Reportable incidents under the Serious Incident Response Scheme (SIRS) are documented, escalated, and notified to the ACQSC within statutory timeframes.
Use of restrictive practices is documented including authorisation, rationale, and review; they are used only as a last resort.
Medication management follows the Aged Care Quality Standards: prescribing, administration, reconciliation, and review are recorded.
Resident agreements, fees, and complaints handling are documented and accessible to the resident and their representative.
Behaviour-support plans are documented for residents whose behaviour places them or others at risk; non-pharmacological strategies are tried first.
Documentation supporting ACQSC assessments — care plans, incident registers, complaints log, governance minutes, training records — is kept current.

clauses.title: Individualised, voice-included care plan
clauses.body: Each resident must have an individualised care plan reviewed at admission, on change of condition, and at least every 6 months — with the resident's voice documented.

clauses.title: SIRS notifications recorded
clauses.body: Reportable incidents under SIRS must be documented, escalated, and notified to ACQSC within statutory timeframes.

clauses.title: Restrictive practices authorised + reviewed
clauses.body: Use of restrictive practices must be documented including authorisation, rationale, and review; used only as a last resort.

clauses.title: Medication management recorded
clauses.body: Prescribing, administration, reconciliation, and review of medication must be recorded per the Aged Care Quality Standards.

clauses.title: Resident agreements + complaints handling
clauses.body: Resident agreements, fees, and complaints handling must be documented and accessible to the resident and their representative.

clauses.title: Behaviour-support plans
clauses.body: Behaviour-support plans must be documented; non-pharmacological strategies tried first.

clauses.title: Assessment-ready documentation
clauses.body: Documentation supporting ACQSC assessments must be kept current — care plans, incident registers, complaints log, governance minutes, training records.
